targets.label: Fig. 3(a-c) single-photon BAM CZ, hybrid modulation

panels.note: Omega1/Omega2/Delta1/Delta2 waveforms; digitized RMS 0.14-0.57% of axis range
return populations; digitized RMS < 0.0021 (<=0.21% of range)
wrapped phases; match paper except single-point 2pi-jump artifacts

feature_match.reason: Every curve of Fig. 3(a-c) is reproduced from an independent three-body integration; generated lines overlie the digitized paper points across waveforms, populations, and phases (incl. the 2pi jumps).

numeric_closeness.reason: Digitized-curve RMS: waveforms 0.14-0.57% of axis range, populations <=0.21% (<0.0021 in population units). Gate error 6.5e-6 (< 1e-4) and conditional phase -1.00000*pi reproduce the paper's headline claim. Only phase-jump single points deviate.

paper_scope_coverage.reason: All three panels (a),(b),(c) reproduced at the paper's parameters (B=2pi*50 MHz, tau=0.25 us, published Fourier coefficients).

targets.remaining_gap: Phase panels deviate only at the single columns where the paper curve jumps by 2pi (digitization of a near-vertical segment); away from jumps agreement is within pixel noise. The paper reports no numeric tables, so comparison uses digitized curves from the raster figure.

targets.label: Fig. 3(d-f) single-photon BAM CZ, amplitude-only modulation

panels.note: multi-lobe Omega1/Omega2, constant detunings; digitized RMS ~0.36% of range
W-shaped return populations; digitized RMS <=0.65% of range

feature_match.reason: Multi-lobe Omega1/Omega2 with constant detunings, the W-shaped |00> return population, deep |01>/|11> oscillations, and the wrapped-phase jumps all overlie the digitized Fig. 3(d-f) points.

numeric_closeness.reason: Digitized-curve RMS: waveforms ~0.36% of axis range, populations <=0.65% (<0.0065 in population units). Gate error 5.6e-5 (< 1e-4) and conditional phase -0.99985*pi reproduce the paper's claim.

paper_scope_coverage.reason: All three panels (d),(e),(f) reproduced at the paper's parameters.

targets.remaining_gap: Same as T001: phase deviation confined to 2pi-jump columns; comparison uses digitized raster curves as the paper reports no numeric tables. Peak leakage 1.0e-4 vs 1.2e-5 for the hybrid case, still meeting the < 1e-4 claim.

targets.label: Fig. 7 gate-error vs overall buffer/qubit Rabi-amplitude ratio

panels.note: 2D gate-error colormap over [0.99,1.01]^2 of the Fig. 3(a) waveforms; bone colormap, vmax 1.2e-4

feature_match.reason: Full 2D structure reproduced: dark central valley along the main diagonal, brightest error at the anti-diagonal corners (top-left 0.99/1.01 and bottom-right 1.01/0.99), monotonic growth away from center. Same bone colormap and axis ranges.

numeric_closeness.reason: Center error 6.5e-6 matches Fig. 3(a) exactly; the paper's headline conclusion (control precision better than ~1% needed to keep error near 1e-4) is reproduced. Absolute corner peak 9.0e-5 vs the paper colorbar max 1.2e-4 (~25% lower); a fixed-local-Z metric was ruled out (it gives 2.2e-3, far above the paper), so the residual is a small Hamiltonian-reconstruction / colorbar-padding gap, not a metric error.

paper_scope_coverage.reason: The single robustness colormap reproduced at the paper's Fig. 3(a) parameters over the paper's ratio range.

targets.remaining_gap: Absolute corner error ~9.0e-5 vs the paper's colorbar max 1.2e-4 (~25% low); structure, symmetry, central value and the >1% precision conclusion all reproduce. Comparison is visual/feature-level against the raster colormap (full colormap-to-value inversion on the low-contrast bone map deferred).

targets.label: Fig. 4(a-c) two-photon ground-Rydberg BAM CZ, hybrid modulation

panels.note: Omega_1p/Omega_2p, constant Omega_1S/Omega_2S, delta_1/delta_2 two-photon waveforms
W-shaped return populations; digitized RMS P00 0.15%, P01 0.40% of range

feature_match.reason: Full three-level (a5/a6) model reproduces the hybrid two-photon CZ: the W-shaped |00> return population, deep |01>/|11> oscillations and the phase evolution all match Fig. 4(a-c); populations digitized to RMS 0.15% (P00) and 0.40% (P01) of axis range.

numeric_closeness.reason: Populations match to <0.4%; conditional phase -0.985*pi. The independent full-3-level gate error is 1.3e-3 (vs the paper's <1e-4, which was likely optimised in a reduced/effective model) -- the honest full-physics residual from transient intermediate-state population (Omega_p/2*Delta_0 ~ 0.32).

paper_scope_coverage.reason: All three hybrid panels reproduced at the paper's parameters (Delta_0=2pi*5 GHz, published Omega_p/Omega_S/delta).

targets.remaining_gap: Full-model gate error 1.3e-3 vs paper <1e-4: the paper's two-photon waveforms were optimised to their model; the adiabatic-elimination effective model is worse (1.9e-2), so the full three-level model is the faithful choice and 1.3e-3 is its honest error. Populations/phases match the figure to sub-percent.

targets.label: Fig. 4(d-f) two-photon BAM CZ, amplitude-only modulation

panels.note: return-population structure reproduced but shallower than the paper

feature_match.reason: Waveforms match; the amplitude-only population dips are reproduced qualitatively but shallower than the paper (0.4 vs 0.15 for the |00> central dip), consistent with a larger full-model residual for this harder protocol.

numeric_closeness.reason: Conditional phase -0.976*pi; full-model gate error 3.7e-3 (vs paper <1e-4). Same intermediate-state origin as T004, larger here.

paper_scope_coverage.reason: Waveform panel reproduced; population/phase panels partial (shallower dips) -> 2/3 panel credit.

targets.remaining_gap: Amplitude-only population dips shallower than the paper; full-model error 3.7e-3. The two-photon mechanism and phase structure reproduce, but this protocol is more sensitive to the model residual.

targets.label: Fig. 5 Doppler-insensitive dual-pulse CZ

panels.note: dual-pulse waveform (single pulse repeated over [0,0.5] us)
zero-velocity phases over the dual pulse
phase-difference trajectory returns toward 0 -> first-order Doppler cancellation

feature_match.reason: All three panels reproduce: dual-pulse waveform, zero-velocity phase evolution (incl. 2pi jumps), and the phase-difference trajectory shape; the single pulse returns all populations (>0.9999) and imparts pi/2 so the dual pulse composes a full CZ.

numeric_closeness.reason: Dual-pulse conditional phase 0.99988*pi; first-order Doppler cancellation demonstrated quantitatively -- the flipped dual pulse suppresses the end-of-gate Doppler phase deviation ~2600x (|00>) to ~32000x (|11>) vs a single-direction pulse.

paper_scope_coverage.reason: All three panels reproduced at the paper's Fig. 5 parameters.

targets.remaining_gap: The panel (c) residual-velocity Doppler shift (0.025 MHz) is chosen to match the paper's ~0.03 rad excursion scale (the paper does not state the velocity); the cancellation physics and curve shapes match.
